reviews.review_msg: An inspiring  movie or documentary before most of the lectures.The tests are quite manageable, if you pay attention to the lecture slides, and also to the textbook. At the end of the course, he curved up the final exam a lot. Nice prof.

You're marked on 2 exams and 1 small paper. 1/3 of lectures have a BBC video, sometimes with David Attenborough. He says that exam content is focused on concepts rather than random facts but he didn't do that with the final exam. Thankfully, he bumped everyone's final exam mark by 14%. Pay attention in class and take notes and you'll be fine.
He is a funny and geed prof, however, the midterm exam and the final are super hard, you must read the textbook and pay attention to lectures in order to pass this course.

I took ARCH 131 with Mark last Summer to fulfill a Breadth requirement. Hes an awesome prof. Very funny and personable. I have now fulfilled an extended minor in Arch because Mark made me want to continue taking Arch classes. The course was comprised of 4 exams, all multiple choice. Go to lectures and study and youll do well.

Mark is a really good prof and I don't really see why there are negative reviews on him. I took this course as intersession and Mark makes the 3hour lectures quite bearable. The exams are so simple and he gives up to 10 marks of bonus on the final. Very engaging and approachable.

Lots of reading and assignments based off the reading. But at least it's online articles and he chose an affordable text. Interesting material, clearly his passion, too much focus on defining the field/its reception for my taste. Fair and quick grader, good feedback, class discussions so do your homework every week to get a good grade.

he's a super funny guy, really nice, and you could tell he was really passionate about his field.. but the class was REALLY hard.. i took it as an elective and regret it.. the exams were super hard and there was a TON of readings. thank god he scaled the exams or else i probably would have failed :/

Mark is obviously very passionate about human origins. I think he would be a great teacher for an upper level course but NOT for a 100 level course. Grading was harsh and tests were hard, each one had to be scaled. If you do take this course GO TO LECTURE and take diligent notes. Don't take this course unless you are interested  in human origins.

This is an example of a prof who shouldn't be teaching 100 level classes. He is obviously very passionate, but impossible to keep up with as a beginner, and his tests/marking rhetoric are very hard and inconsistent. The course was trying to do too much, and in the process he has likely turned a lot of eager students off of human origins.

I strongly, STRONGLY, advise others not to take this course. Even when you study hard it's extremely difficult to get a good grade. It was never clear what we should study. Some questions on the exams seemed to come out of nowhere, and thats WITH studying my butt off!  Very difficult, certainly challenged me...

A good prof, but his exams are too bloody hard. It's a 1styear course, he shouldn't expect us to memorize impossibly long latin names for a million different species and body parts.  Luckily the final wasn't cumulative.  Lectures were a hit or miss, but I liked his videos

I took the course this semester because I found the course title interesting. Just after the first mid-term, it hit me hard that I had done a mistake. I had read all the chapters, and studied quite well for the exam, but I scored 53% with a class average of 58%. Don't take this course if your GPA is not good. Trust me you are going to repent it!

I am an Arts student that took this course because of the suggestion that it would be a good arts-science. It wasn't. We've read the entire textbook (with absolutely no mention of what we're supposed to focus on IN the textbook). 3 hour lectures once a week, no tutorials. This class is incredibly difficult. Don't do it!

His lectures are interesting and the assignment summaries are okay, but the midterms are a bit off topic. The lecture slides and content mentioned in class only make up half of what's in the midterm. Not sure where the rest comes from.
Such a funny dude, and the tests were pretty straightforward. I plan to see what other classes he teaches so I can take them too!
This guy is awesome! Don't let the haters keep you from taking his classes. This prof knows his stuff and if you are prepared to work hard you will do well.

A really great prof overall. It's easy to tell that he loves what he does and his passion makes you a lot more interested in what he's teaching. Granted, you really do have to attend lecture and keep up with the readings, if you do that you'll pass the class easily. Go see the TAs if you're worried!

One of the hardest B-Sci courses i've taken. Alot of heavy memorization. Final exam covers the ENTIRE TEXTBOOK. that's 19 chapters.. Lectures are boring as hell.. and there are alot of names you have to memorize like "Australopithecus". bla bla bla.. Wouldn't recommend this class.. HARD 1st year class! Avoid this class if you can
He was able to keep my interest and got me excited about learning more.

I found this course to be thoroughly enjoyable. The content was interesting, and the lectures were easy to understand and posted online. Prof. Collard was approachable and made a special effort to answer questions during lectures. The tests were also not too difficult, and there was plenty of time to complete the article review.

His career is archaeology so he seemed to expect that we were all future arch majors and had a deep interest in the course. Expected way too much and his tests were extremely difficult for a first year course. I still got an A but it was not easy.
Class too difficult for first year, but the material was interesting, overall not amazing but a good choice for a B-Sci course

Huge amount of material to get through, the entire textbook, hence 4 exams and article review. Prof wasn't too bad in lectures, his slides were informative and he took the time to work on them. Approachable and friendly in lectures and out, sense of humor. Nice guy. Decent course, read and you will do well, as exams are multiple choice.

Mark is a good prof.  His exams are fair and he's a good lecturer.  Unfortunately, the course is so bland and boring that no one enjoyed the lectures and it's hard to do well on exams because you tune out after awhile and stop paying attention the the lecture material.  Still, if u need a B-Sci course this one is not too bad.

The worst class I've ever had...extremely dry materials and u will have no intention to go to the class after a few weeks. He just read exactly the same from his slides and he never put them on the webct in advance! The text is bad either, even he gives u the essay questions in advance, it doesn't mean that u can get the answers from the text!

the only reason why i signed up on ratemyprof was to give my review of this guy. he is the worst prof i ever had in my life. he's lecture notes are crap. his tests are outrageously hard, with frequent questions that had nothing to do with course topic. his 1 page Article Review (worth 40%) is marked incredibly hard. he should be kicked out of sfu.
Course had a ridiculous amount of material to know. Tended to test what seemed unimportant (History - who discovered what and when - vs. why this discovery matters.)
very engaged, cares for his students. social skills need improvement (very aprupt and appears rude at times). BUT: you learn something for sure!! :)
He expects too much for a first year course. His lectures are boring and more than half the class leaves at the break. Do not take this class!

Boring lectures that don't correspond very well to the text book and lots of memorizing.  Asked trivial questions on the midterm that didn't have much to do with the course content.  Final was easier though because he gave you the essay questions beforehand. Don't leave, talk or text during class unless you want to be called out infront of everyone

Hardest first year course I've ever taken. Unrealistic expectations for the final exam and too much memorization. Lectures were sometimes very dry. Be prepared to read...a LOT! And bring that reading knowledge with you into the cumulative final exam.

Interesting course, but definitely require a lot of reading and work. Grade consist of midterm (MC), review article and written final. Lectures are very heavy in content and sometimes boring. Slides are posted on WebCT, but not until while after. Gave class list of 8 essay Qs to study for final. Final then consisted of 3 random Qs out of the 8.
Do not take this class.  Bad teacher and bad material. Way too hard for first year.  Outside sources and in text citations on a final exam for a first year class?
